---
title: "Mutual Information and Diffusion Processes — Backup"
author: "**Giulio Franzese** — Eurecom, Data Science Department"
date: ""
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    scrollable: true
    smaller: true
    fig-align: center
    transition: slide
    chalkboard: false
    controls: true
    html-math-method:
      method: mathjax
      url: "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-chtml-full.js"
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: assets/logo/eurecom.png
      data-background-size: "18%"
      data-background-position: "bottom 30px right 40px"
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <style>
    .bio-conclusion {
      background-color: #E8F4FD;
      border-left: 3px solid #4A90D9;
      color: #1a3a5c;
      padding: 0.4em 0.8em;
      border-radius: 4px;
      margin: 0.4em 0;
      font-size: 0.92em;
    }
    .bio-conclusion strong { color: #2c6fad; }
    </style>
    <script>
    window.MathJax = {
      tex: {
        tags: 'ams',
        macros: {
          textsc:  ["{\\text{#1}}", 1],
          mathbold:    ["{\\boldsymbol{{#1}}}", 1],
          mba: "{\\boldsymbol{a}}", mbb: "{\\boldsymbol{b}}", mbc: "{\\boldsymbol{c}}",
          mbd: "{\\boldsymbol{d}}", mbe: "{\\boldsymbol{e}}", mbf: "{\\boldsymbol{f}}",
          mbg: "{\\boldsymbol{g}}", mbh: "{\\boldsymbol{h}}", mbi: "{\\boldsymbol{i}}",
          mbj: "{\\boldsymbol{j}}", mbk: "{\\boldsymbol{k}}", mbl: "{\\boldsymbol{l}}",
          mbm: "{\\boldsymbol{m}}", mbn: "{\\boldsymbol{n}}", mbo: "{\\boldsymbol{o}}",
          mbp: "{\\boldsymbol{p}}", mbq: "{\\boldsymbol{q}}", mbr: "{\\boldsymbol{r}}",
          mbs: "{\\boldsymbol{s}}", mbt: "{\\boldsymbol{t}}", mbu: "{\\boldsymbol{u}}",
          mbv: "{\\boldsymbol{v}}", mbw: "{\\boldsymbol{w}}", mbx: "{\\boldsymbol{x}}",
          mby: "{\\boldsymbol{y}}", mbz: "{\\boldsymbol{z}}",
          mbA: "{\\boldsymbol{A}}", mbB: "{\\boldsymbol{B}}", mbC: "{\\boldsymbol{C}}",
          mbD: "{\\boldsymbol{D}}", mbE: "{\\boldsymbol{E}}", mbF: "{\\boldsymbol{F}}",
          mbG: "{\\boldsymbol{G}}", mbH: "{\\boldsymbol{H}}", mbI: "{\\boldsymbol{I}}",
          mbJ: "{\\boldsymbol{J}}", mbK: "{\\boldsymbol{K}}", mbL: "{\\boldsymbol{L}}",
          mbM: "{\\boldsymbol{M}}", mbN: "{\\boldsymbol{N}}", mbO: "{\\boldsymbol{O}}",
          mbP: "{\\boldsymbol{P}}", mbQ: "{\\boldsymbol{Q}}", mbR: "{\\boldsymbol{R}}",
          mbS: "{\\boldsymbol{S}}", mbT: "{\\boldsymbol{T}}", mbU: "{\\boldsymbol{U}}",
          mbV: "{\\boldsymbol{V}}", mbW: "{\\boldsymbol{W}}", mbX: "{\\boldsymbol{X}}",
          mbY: "{\\boldsymbol{Y}}", mbZ: "{\\boldsymbol{Z}}",
          mbalpha:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\alpha}}",
          mbbeta:    "{\\boldsymbol{\\beta}}",
          mbgamma:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\gamma}}",
          mbdelta:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\delta}}",
          mbepsilon: "{\\boldsymbol{\\epsilon}}",
          mbchi:     "{\\boldsymbol{\\chi}}",
          mbeta:     "{\\boldsymbol{\\eta}}",
          mbiota:    "{\\boldsymbol{\\iota}}",
          mbkappa:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\kappa}}",
          mblambda:  "{\\boldsymbol{\\lambda}}",
          mbmu:      "{\\boldsymbol{\\mu}}",
          mbnu:      "{\\boldsymbol{\\nu}}",
          mbomega:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\omega}}",
          mbphi:     "{\\boldsymbol{\\phi}}",
          mbpi:      "{\\boldsymbol{\\pi}}",
          mbpsi:     "{\\boldsymbol{\\psi}}",
          mbrho:     "{\\boldsymbol{\\rho}}",
          mbsigma:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\sigma}}",
          mbtau:     "{\\boldsymbol{\\tau}}",
          mbtheta:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\theta}}",
          mbupsilon: "{\\boldsymbol{\\upsilon}}",
          mbvarepsilon: "{\\boldsymbol{\\varepsilon}}",
          mbvarphi:  "{\\boldsymbol{\\varphi}}",
          mbvartheta:"{\\boldsymbol{\\vartheta}}",
          mbvarrho:  "{\\boldsymbol{\\varrho}}",
          mbxi:      "{\\boldsymbol{\\xi}}",
          mbzeta:    "{\\boldsymbol{\\zeta}}",
          mbDelta:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\Delta}}",
          mbGamma:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\Gamma}}",
          mbLambda:  "{\\boldsymbol{\\Lambda}}",
          mbOmega:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\Omega}}",
          mbPhi:     "{\\boldsymbol{\\Phi}}",
          mbPi:      "{\\boldsymbol{\\Pi}}",
          mbPsi:     "{\\boldsymbol{\\Psi}}",
          mbSigma:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\Sigma}}",
          mbTheta:   "{\\boldsymbol{\\Theta}}",
          mbUpsilon: "{\\boldsymbol{\\Upsilon}}",
          mbXi:      "{\\boldsymbol{\\Xi}}",
          qL: "{\\mathbb{Q}}",
          pL: "{\\mathbb{P}}",
          KL:   ["{\\text{KL}\\!\\left[#1\\;\\|\\;#2\\right]}", 2],
          Df:   ["{\\mathcal{D}_f\\!\\left[#1\\;\\|\\;#2\\right]}", 2],
          Gauss: ["{\\mathcal{N}_{#1}}", 1],
          E:    "{\\mathbb{E}}",
          bbH:  "{\\mathbb{H}}",
          bbR:  "{\\mathbb{R}}",
          bbS:  "{\\mathbb{S}}",
          cB: "{\\mathcal{B}}", cC: "{\\mathcal{C}}", cS: "{\\mathcal{S}}",
          cD: "{\\mathcal{D}}", cL: "{\\mathcal{L}}", cN: "{\\mathcal{N}}",
          cP: "{\\mathcal{P}}", cQ: "{\\mathcal{Q}}", cR: "{\\mathcal{R}}",
          cF: "{\\mathcal{F}}", cI: "{\\mathcal{I}}", cT: "{\\mathcal{T}}",
          cV: "{\\mathcal{V}}", cE: "{\\mathcal{E}}", cG: "{\\mathcal{G}}",
          cX: "{\\mathcal{X}}", cY: "{\\mathcal{Y}}", cH: "{\\mathcal{H}}",
          cW: "{\\mathcal{W}}", cU: "{\\mathcal{U}}",
          Normal: "{\\mathcal{N}}",
          argmax: "{\\operatorname*{arg\\,max}}",
          argmin: "{\\operatorname*{arg\\,min}}",
          defeq:  "{\\stackrel{\\scriptscriptstyle\\text{def}}{=}}",
          d:      ["{\\operatorname{d}\\!{#1}}", 1],
          dd:     "{\\operatorname{d}\\!}",
          g:      "{\\,|\\,}",
          scalprod: ["{\\langle #1,\\, #2\\rangle}", 2],
          norm:   ["{\\left\\| #1 \\right\\|}", 1],
          const:  "{\\mathrm{const.}}",
          diag:   "{\\textrm{diag}}",
          supp:   "{\\textrm{supp}}",
          pdata:  "{p_{\\text{data}}}",
          fft:    "{\\mathfrak{F}}",
          ifft:   "{\\mathfrak{I}}",
          inv:    "{-1}",
          eye:    "{\\boldsymbol{I}}",
          zerovect: "{\\mathbf{0}}",
          Lelbo:  "{\\mathcal{L}_{\\text{ELBO}}}",
          bigO:   "{\\mathcal{O}}"
        }
      }
    };
    </script>
---

## An Informal Introduction

::: {style="font-size: 2em; text-align: center; margin-top: 2em;"}
An **informal** introduction
:::

---

## Probability Spaces

- A probability space $(\Omega, \mathcal{F}, \mathrm{P})$ consists of:
  - $\Omega$: The sample space (set of all possible outcomes).
  - $\mathcal{F}$: A $\sigma$-algebra, a collection of subsets of $\Omega$ representing events.
  - $\mathrm{P}$: A probability measure that assigns a number between 0 and 1 to each event in $\mathcal{F}$.

---

## Understanding $\sigma$-Algebra in Simple Terms

- A $\sigma$-algebra can be understood as defining the **granularity** of information available.
- It must satisfy:
  - The whole sample space $\Omega$ is included.
  - If an event is in $\mathcal{F}$, its complement must also be in $\mathcal{F}$.
  - If a countable sequence of events is in $\mathcal{F}$, their union must also be in $\mathcal{F}$.
- Example: Rolling a dice.
  - $\Omega = \{1,2,3,4,5,6\}$.
  - A possible $\sigma$-algebra: $\{\emptyset, \Omega, \{1,3,5\}, \{2,4,6\}\}$ (representing even and odd outcomes).

---

## Understanding $\sigma$-Algebra (cont.)

![Example of $\sigma$-algebra](figures/sigmaalg.png){width=50%}

---

## Random Variables as Mappings

- A random variable $X$ is a function that maps outcomes from the sample space $\Omega$ to real numbers:
$$X: \Omega \to \mathbb{R}$$
- Example 1: Rolling a dice. If $\omega\in\Omega=\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}$, then $X(\omega)=\omega$.
- Example 2: Defining an odd/even variable. $Y(\omega)=1$ if $\omega$ is odd, $Y(\omega)=0$ if $\omega$ is even.

---

## Random Variables as Mappings (cont.)

- A random variable must be measurable with respect to a given $\sigma$-algebra to be well-defined.
- Example:
  - With $\sigma$-algebra $\{\emptyset,\Omega,\{1,3,5\},\{2,4,6\}\}$, $Y$ (odd/even) is measurable.
  - However, $X$ (exact roll outcome) is not measurable — a **finer** $\sigma$-algebra is needed.
- Intuitive interpretation: **Doob's lemma**: A random variable is measurable with respect to a $\sigma$-algebra if and only if there exists a function that depends only on elements of the algebra.

---

## Introduction to Stochastic Processes

- A stochastic process is a collection of random variables indexed by time:
$$Y_t: \Omega \to \mathbb{R}, \quad t \geq 0$$
- It describes how a system evolves randomly over time.
- Examples:
  - Stock prices.
  - Population dynamics.
  - Temperature variations.

---

## Filtration and Adapted Processes

- A filtration is a sequence of $\sigma$-algebras representing growing information over time:
$$\mathcal{F}_0 \subseteq \mathcal{F}_1 \subseteq \mathcal{F}_2 \subseteq \dots$$
- Intuitively, it models the **accumulation of knowledge** as time progresses.
- A process is *adapted* if at each time instant it is measurable with respect to the filtration.

---

## Probability Measure

(**Informal, not complete**) In the framework of measure theory, a probability measure $\mu$ is a mapping from **subsets** $\{S_i\}_i$ of a given sample space $\Omega$ such that:

- It is positive: $\mu(S_i)\geq 0$
- For disjoint sets $S_i,S_j$ ($S_i\cap S_j=\emptyset$) it holds $\mu(S_i\cup S_j)=\mu(S_i)+\mu(S_j)$.
- Its "integral" is equal to one: $\mu(\Omega)=1$.

Familiar examples:

- $\Omega=\mathbb{R}$, Gaussian probability $\mu(dx)=p(x)dx=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\exp(-\frac{x^2}{2})dx$
- $\Omega=\{0,1\}$, Bernoulli RV $\mu(\{0\})=\mu(\{1\})=\frac{1}{2}$

---

## Definition of SDEs

- A stochastic differential equation (SDE) is a particular class of stochastic processes.
- A general diffusion process on a probability space $(\Omega,\mathcal{F},\mathrm{P})$ is given by:
$$Y_t(\omega) = Y_0(\omega) + \int_0^t H(Y_s(\omega),t)\,ds + W_t(\omega)$$
where:
  - $Y_t$ is the stochastic process.
  - $H(Y_t(\omega),t)$ is the drift term.
  - $W_t$ is a Brownian motion, representing Gaussian noise.
- It only makes sense when considered with respect to a **filtration** $(\mathcal{F}_t)_{t\geq 0}$, representing the available information over time.

---

## Formal MINDE Derivation

- Consider a probability space $(\Omega,\mathcal{F},\mathbb{P})$, together with a growing filtration $\mathcal{F}_t$
- Consider a generic random variable $A$ ($\mathcal{F}_0$-measurable) with probability measure $\mu^A(dx)$
- Build a simple SDE in $[0,T]$ with initial conditions $\sim\mu^A$:
$$\begin{cases}\dd X_t=-X_t\,\dd t+\sqrt{2}\,\dd W_t,\\X_0=A\sim\mu^A\end{cases}$$
where $W_t$ is an $\mathcal{F}_t$ Brownian motion.
- This SDE corresponds to a path measure $\mathbb{P}^{A}$.
- Two SDEs which differ only by initial conditions have KL divergence:
$$\KL{\mathbb{P}^{\mu^A}}{\mathbb{P}^{\mu^B}}=\E_{\mathbb{P}^{\mu^A}}\!\left[\log\frac{d\mathbb{P}^{\mu^A}}{d\mathbb{P}^{\mu^B}}\right]=\KL{\mu^A}{\mu^B}$$

---

## Formal MINDE Derivation (cont.)

**Time-reversal** $\hat{X}_t\defeq X_{T-t}$:

- KL is invariant to time-reversal: $\KL{\mathbb{P}^{\mu^A}}{\mathbb{P}^{\mu^B}}=\KL{\hat{\mathbb{P}}^{\mu^A}}{\hat{\mathbb{P}}^{\mu^B}}$
- Time reversal of SDE is again an SDE [Léonard 2014]
- Score function $\leftrightarrow$ Denoiser (same as before)

**Girsanov theorem**:

$$\KL{\hat{\mathbb{P}}^{\mu^A}}{\hat{\mathbb{P}}^{\mu^B}}\simeq \E_{\mathbb{P}^{\mu^A}}\!\left[\int_0^T\norm{\nabla\log p^{A}_t(X_t)-\nabla\log p^{B}_t(X_t)}^2\dd t\right]$$

- Combining with time-reversal invariance → KL-estimator!
- Initial conditions $\KL{\mu^A_T}{\mu^B_T}$ should be negligible (Bakry-Emery condition / ergodicity [Collet, Malrieu 2008])

---

## Experimental Results

MINDE variants:

- Consistently outperform all competitors on standard benchmarks.
- Are capable of accurately estimating MI in the challenging High MI benchmarks:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="30%"}
![Sparse Normal](figures/high_mi_multinormal.jpg){width=100%}
:::
::: {.column width="30%"}
![Half-cube](figures/Half-cube.jpg){width=100%}
:::
::: {.column width="30%"}
![Spiral](figures/spiral.jpg){width=100%}
:::
:::

---

## Experimental Results (cont.)

Pass the self-consistency tests:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
![Baseline](figures/consistency.jpg){width=100%}
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
![Data Processing](figures/dataprocessing.jpg){width=100%}
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
![Additivity](figures/additivity.jpg){width=100%}
:::
:::

![](figures/consistency_tests.PNG){width=100%}

![](figures/minde_res.PNG){width=60%}
